subject: [PATCH v4 09/11] mm: handle PMD swap entry faults on swap-in

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch adds a handler for PMD swap entry faults on swap-in, allowing the kernel to resolve entire 2 MB mappings in one shot. It introduces a new function `do_huge_pmd_swap_page()` that handles the fault and mirrors the behavior of `do_swap_page()` at PTE granularity. The handler looks up the folio in the swap cache, allocates a PMD-order folio if necessary, and re-validates the folio after locking. It also checks for hardware-poisoned subpages, restores soft_dirty and uffd_wp flags, and maps writable only when permitted. If the resulting PMD is read-only but the fault was a write, it updates `vmf->orig_pmd` and calls `wp_huge_pmd()` to COW immediately. The patch also adds logic to split the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries if PMD-order resources are unavailable.
